top_candidates.reasoning: The engineering layer literally quotes 'kutas tvā kaśmalam idam viṣame samupasthitam' inside the concrete scenario — that is BG 2.2 verbatim. Even without the quote, the diagnostic-question form, the senior-correcting-senior register, and the named anārya-juṣṭam-style triad (unworthy of noble / not your record / harms team) match BG 2.2's structure: kutas + kaśmalam + viṣame + anārya-juṣṭam + akīrti-karam.

BG 2.3 is the next verse and contains the corrective imperative ('uttiṣṭha — stand up'). Some of the engineering layer's tone ('drop the costume immediately and stand up' would actually be 2.3, not 2.2 — but the analog here is squarely on diagnosis, not imperative). Distantly possible but the diagnostic-question structure is 2.2.
Distant. 18.66 is integrative ('release all dharma'), not diagnostic. Included only as a low-confidence sanity third.
